Overview

Address
812 SE 48TH AVENUE, PORTLAND, OR 97215
Phone
5032362624

37 (91% of beds filled)

Setting
Urban
2 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 2/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 2/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 3/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 2/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 2/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures — Data not available.

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 5.23 3.83 5.27 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.49 0.67 0.50 ≥ 0.55 Below floor
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 1.26 0.85 1.27 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 3.48 2.31 3.51 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.75 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.00 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
4.86
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.46
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
4.90
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
98.2%
annual
RN turnover
80.0%
annual
Administrators departed
0
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.36
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.98
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-02-28 16 16 7 80 1 80
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2023-12-01 18 10 8 112 1 112
Total weighted health score
88.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (47)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

46 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
1 G/H/I Actual harm
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0565 Honor the resident's right to organize and participate in resident/family groups in the facility. E 2025-02-28 2025-04-18
0576 Ensure residents have reasonable access to and privacy in their use of communication methods. E 2025-02-28 2025-04-18
0585 Honor the resident's right to voice grievances without discrimination or reprisal and the facility must establish a grievance policy and make prompt efforts to resolve grievances. D 2025-02-28 2025-04-18
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. D 2025-02-28 2025-04-18
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2025-02-28 2025-04-18
0645 PASARR screening for Mental disorders or Intellectual Disabilities D 2025-02-28 2025-04-18
0699 Provide care or services that was trauma informed and/or culturally competent. D 2025-02-28 2025-04-18
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2025-02-28 2025-04-18
0909 Regularly inspect all bed frames, mattresses, and bed rails (if any) for safety; and all bed rails and mattresses must attach safely to the bed frame. D 2025-02-28 2025-04-18
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. D 2025-02-28 2025-04-18
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. D 2025-02-28 2025-04-18
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2025-02-28 2025-04-18
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2025-02-28 2025-04-18
0679 Provide activities to meet all resident's needs. D 2025-02-28 2025-04-18
0745 Provide medically-related social services to help each resident achieve the highest possible quality of life. E 2025-02-28 2025-04-18
0791 Provide or obtain dental services for each resident. D 2025-02-28 2025-04-18
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2025-02-28 2025-04-18
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. D 2024-07-25 2024-08-30
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2024-07-25 2024-08-30
0814 Dispose of garbage and refuse properly. E 2024-07-25 2024-08-30
0555 Honor the resident's right to choose his or her attending physician. D 2024-03-11 2024-04-05
0679 Provide activities to meet all resident's needs. D 2023-12-01 2024-01-20
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2023-12-01 2024-01-20
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2023-12-01 2024-01-20
0698 Provide safe, appropriate dialysis care/services for a resident who requires such services. D 2023-12-01 2024-01-20
0727 Have a registered nurse on duty 8 hours a day; and select a registered nurse to be the director of nurses on a full time basis. E 2023-12-01 2024-01-20
0730 Observe each nurse aide's job performance and give regular training. E 2023-12-01 2024-01-20
0801 Employ sufficient staff with the appropriate competencies and skills sets to carry out the functions of the food and nutrition service, including a qualified dietician. E 2023-12-01 2024-01-20
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2023-12-01 2024-01-20
0825 Provide or get specialized rehabilitative services as required for a resident. D 2023-12-01 2024-01-20
0947 Ensure nurse aides have the skills they need to care for residents, and give nurse aides education in dementia care and abuse prevention. E 2023-12-01 2024-01-20
0583 Keep residents' personal and medical records private and confidential. D 2023-05-23 2023-06-28
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. E 2023-05-23 2023-06-28
0655 Create and put into place a plan for meeting the resident's most immediate needs within 48 hours of being admitted D 2023-05-23 2023-06-28
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. E 2023-05-23 2023-06-28
0679 Provide activities to meet all resident's needs. D 2023-05-23 2023-06-28
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. D 2023-05-23 2023-06-28
0727 Have a registered nurse on duty 8 hours a day; and select a registered nurse to be the director of nurses on a full time basis. E 2023-05-23 2023-06-28
0791 Provide or obtain dental services for each resident. D 2023-05-23 2023-06-28
0809 Ensure meals and snacks are served at times in accordance with resident’s needs, preferences, and requests. Suitable and nourishing alternative meals and snacks must be provided for residents who want to eat at non-traditional times or outside of scheduled meal times. D 2023-05-23 2023-06-28
0810 Provide special eating equipment and utensils for residents who need them and appropriate assistance. D 2023-05-23 2023-06-28
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2023-05-23 2023-06-28
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2023-05-23 2023-06-28
0924 Put firmly secured handrails on each side of hallways. E 2023-05-23 2023-06-28
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. D 2023-05-23 2023-06-28
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2023-05-23 2023-06-28
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. G 2023-05-23 2023-06-28
